name: re-asserting a relationship after negation must not leave a negated zombie
description: Adversarial assertion-transition probe. Note 1 negates the worksFor edge ('Bjorn no longer works at Acme', assertion=negated). Note 2 re-asserts it ('Bjorn is back at Acme', assertion=asserted) — same functional predicate, same object entity Acme. supersession.values_equal keys on object_entity_id only for a pure edge, so the asserted candidate is 'equal' to the still-active negated row and decide returns refresh_id: the pipeline refreshes the NEGATED row in place (statement/confidence) and NEVER updates its assertion. Result: the only worksFor row stays assertion=negated despite the latest note asserting the job — a negated zombie that reads as 'not working there' when the truth is the opposite. SPEC-CORRECT expectation encoded: an active ASSERTED worksFor to Acme after re-assertion, and NO active negated one. XFAIL: refresh_id path ignores assertion, so the active row is still negated. Failing line: "expected a fact matching {...'assertion': 'asserted'...}, found none".
xfail: Assertion transitions are invisible to supersession: decide()'s idempotency refresh (values_equal true + status active) matches a negated row when a later note re-asserts the same edge, and _upsert_fact's refresh branch updates statement/confidence but not assertion — so a negated->asserted flip leaves the row negated (no zombie-clearing on re-assertion).

steps.body: Bjorn no longer works at Acme — he left last month.

facts.statement: Bjorn no longer works at Acme.

steps.body: Bjorn is back at Acme again — rehired this week.
